### Run Command (Background) - Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background and returns a handle for further interaction. Supports environment variables, user context, working directory, and stdout/stderr callbacks. Includes command execution timeout and request timeout.

```python
class Commands():
    @overload
    async def run(self, cmd: str,
                  background: Literal[True],
                  envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                  user: Username = "user",
                  cwd: Optional[str] = None,
                  on_stdout: Optional[OutputHandler[Stdout]] = None,
                  on_stderr: Optional[OutputHandler[Stderr]] = None,
                  timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
                  request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle:
        # Implementation omitted for brevity
```

--------------------------------

### Run commands in background (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/commands/background/page.mdx

Execute a command in the background using the `commands.run()` method with the `background: true` option. The command continues to run after the method returns. It can be terminated later using `command.kill()`. The `onStdout` callback receives data as it's produced.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

// Start the command in the background
const command = await sandbox.commands.run('echo hello; sleep 10; echo world', {
  background: true, // $HighlightLine
  onStdout: (data) => {
    console.log(data)
  },
})

// Kill the command
await command.kill()
```

--------------------------------

### Python: Run Command in Background

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a command in the background within the sandbox environment. This overload of the `run` function returns a `CommandHandle` object immediately, allowing asynchronous interaction with the running process. Environment variables, user, and working directory can be configured. Timeouts for the command connection and the request can also be specified.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict, Literal
from e2b_dev.sandbox.command import CommandHandle, Username

# Assuming 'sandbox' is an initialized object with the commands module
sandbox.commands.run(cmd: str,
                    background: Literal[True],
                    envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                    user: Username = "user",
                    cwd: Optional[str] = None,
                    on_stdout: None = None,
                    on_stderr: None = None,
                    timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
                    request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

### Run Command (Background) - Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.4/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a command in the background and returns a handle for interaction. It takes the command string, environment variables, user, working directory, and timeouts. The `background` parameter must be set to `True`. Returns a CommandHandle object.

```python
from typing import Dict, Optional, Union, Literal
from e2b import Username, CommandHandle

@overload
def run(cmd: str,
        background: Literal[True],
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        on_stdout: None = None,
        on_stderr: None = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

### Run Command in Background (Asynchronous)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.6.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background and returns a CommandHandle for interaction. Supports environment variables, user specification, and working directory. Includes timeouts for command connection and the request itself. Callbacks for stdout/stderr are not used in this mode.

```python
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        on_stdout: None = None,
        on_stderr: None = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle
```

Starts a new command in the background and immediately returns a `CommandHandle` object for interaction. This allows for asynchronous command execution. Options include environment variables, user, working directory, and timeouts. Stdout/stderr callbacks are not applicable in this mode.

```python

def run(cmd: str,

        on_stderr: None = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
```

### Run Command in Background and Get Handle using Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

This overload of the `run` function starts a new command in the background and returns an `AsyncCommandHandle` for interaction. It requires the command string and `background=True`. Other parameters include environment variables, user, working directory, output handlers, and timeouts.

```python
@overload
async def run(cmd: str,
              background: Literal[True],
              envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
              user: Username = "user",
              cwd: Optional[str] = None,
              on_stdout: Optional[OutputHandler[Stdout]] = None,
              on_stderr: Optional[OutputHandler[Stderr]] = None,
              timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
              request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle:
    """
    Start a new command and return a handle to interact with it.

    **Arguments**:

    - `cmd`: Command to execute
    - `background`: `False` if the command should be executed in the foreground, **`True` if the command should be executed in the background**
    - `envs`: Environment variables used for the command
    - `user`: User to run the command as
    - `cwd`: Working directory to run the command
    - `on_stdout`: Callback for command stdout output
    - `on_stderr`: Callback for command stderr output
    - `timeout`: Timeout for the command connection in **seconds**. Using `0` will not limit the command connection time
    - `request_timeout`: Timeout for the request in **seconds**

    **Returns**:

    `AsyncCommandHandle` handle to interact with the running command
    """
    pass
```

### Run Command in Foreground (Synchronous)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.6.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new command and waits for its execution to complete, returning a CommandResult. Supports background execution, environment variables, user specification, working directory, and callbacks for stdout/stderr. Includes timeouts for command connection and the request itself.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict, Callable, Union, Literal
from e2b.sandbox.models import CommandResult, Username

@overload
def run(cmd: str,
        background: Union[Literal[False], None] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        user: Username = "user",
        cwd: Optional[str] = None,
        on_stdout: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
        on_stderr: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
        timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
        request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandResult
```

--------------------------------

### Run commands in background (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/commands/background/page.mdx

Initiate a command in the background by setting `background=True` in the `sandbox.commands.run()` method. The command executes independently, and its output can be iterated upon or retrieved. Use `command.kill()` to terminate the process. The example shows iterating through stdout and stderr.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create()

# Start the command in the background
command = sandbox.commands.run('echo hello; sleep 10; echo world', background=True)

# Get stdout and stderr from the command running in the background.
# You can run this code in a separate thread or use command.wait() to wait for the command to finish.
for stdout, stderr, _ in command:
    if stdout:
        print(stdout)
    if stderr:
        print(stderr)

# Kill the command
command.kill()
```

--------------------------------

### Run Command in Background (Asynchronous) with Commands API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Executes a command in the background without waiting for completion. Returns a CommandHandle object for interaction. Supports environment variables, user, working directory, and timeouts.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict, Union, Literal

# Assuming CommandHandle, Username are defined elsewhere

class Commands:
    @overload
    def run(self, cmd: str,
            background: Literal[True],
            envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
            user: Username = "user",
            cwd: Optional[str] = None,
            on_stdout: None = None,
            on_stderr: None = None,
            timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
            request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> CommandHandle:
        """Start a new command and return a handle to interact with it."""
        pass  # Implementation details omitted
```

### Run Command in Background and Get Handle in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.2/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background and returns an AsyncCommandHandle for interaction. This handle allows for asynchronous operations like waiting for completion or managing I/O. It supports environment variables, user context, and output handlers. A timeout can be specified for the command connection.

```python
@overload
async def run(cmd: str,
              background: Literal[True],
              envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
              user: Username = "user",
              cwd: Optional[str] = None,
              on_stdout: Optional[OutputHandler[Stdout]] = None,
              on_stderr: Optional[OutputHandler[Stderr]] = None,
              timeout: Optional[float] = 60,
              request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> AsyncCommandHandle:
    """Start a new command and return a handle to interact with it."""
    pass
```
